skill.name: academic-norm-review
skill.description: Detects content similarity, verifies standardized citations and abbreviations, and flags potential academic integrity risks; use it before submission, during academic writing QA, or for compliance reviews.
skill.summary: Reviews academic manuscripts for citation consistency, abbreviation standards, and potential integrity risks before submission.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 26 static analysis findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings match metadata about security scores in the audit JSON and ordinary prose in SKILL.md, not actual cryptographic operations. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings correspond to markdown code fence delimiters in a documentation-only skill that has no executable code. The 'hardcoded URL' is a legitimate source attribution link. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or credential harvesting patterns were detected. The skill is documentation-driven and poses no security risk.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm - false positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at 9 locations. All are false positives: lines in audit_result_v1.json are metadata about security scoring (score 11/12, not crypto), and lines in SKILL.md are ordinary prose describing the skill, suggestions, and documentation requirements. No cryptographic operations exist in this documentation-only skill.

low_findings.title: Ruby/shell backtick execution - false positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 15 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. All correspond to markdown code fence delimiters (```text, ```markdown), not actual shell execution backticks. The skill explicitly states 'None (documentation-driven skill)' under Dependencies and contains no executable Ruby or shell code.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance - false positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'system reconnaissance' at 2 locations. Line 190 in audit_result_v1.json is a note about document scope boundaries. Line 121 in SKILL.md describes abbreviation consistency rules. Neither relates to system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL - false positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 2 instances of 'hardcoded URL' at SKILL.md:7. This is a legitimate source attribution link pointing to the skill's GitHub repository. It is not a network call or data exfiltration target.

content.user_title: Review academic norms in manuscripts for compliance
content.value_statement: Academic manuscripts often contain citation inconsistencies, undefined abbreviations, and repetitive phrasing that can delay publication or raise integrity concerns. This skill systematically reviews your manuscript against academic standards and produces a prioritized compliance report with location-specific revision suggestions.
actual_capabilities: Verifies citation formatting consistency and completeness across the manuscript
Detects undefined abbreviations and flags inconsistent abbreviation usage
Identifies high-similarity passages and repetitive phrasing within the document
Validates resolvability between in-text citations and reference list entries
Generates a prioritized issue list with locations and revision suggestions
Produces structured compliance reports using provided checklist templates
limitations: Cannot check against external databases for plagiarism or similarity scoring
Cannot automatically correct issues, only flag them for human review and revision
Requires the full manuscript text to be provided as input within the conversation
Does not support proprietary or non-standard citation formats beyond common academic styles

use_cases.title: Pre-submission compliance screening
use_cases.description: Run a final review of a manuscript before journal or conference submission to catch citation, abbreviation, and formatting issues.

use_cases.title: Multi-author draft consistency check
use_cases.description: Review a manuscript with contributions from multiple authors to ensure consistent citation styles and abbreviation definitions across all sections.
use_cases.target_user: Academic editors and research group leads

use_cases.title: Institutional integrity audit
use_cases.description: Flag high-similarity passages and potential academic integrity risks in internal compliance reviews for institutional audits.
use_cases.target_user: Institutional review officers and ethics committees

prompt_templates.title: Basic manuscript compliance check
prompt_templates.prompt: Please review the following manuscript text for academic norm compliance. Check citation consistency, undefined abbreviations, and repetitive phrasing. Use the checklist from assets/academic_compliance_checklist.md to structure your output.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user submitting a single-author paper draft

prompt_templates.title: Citation format verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the citations in this manuscript. Check that in-text citations use a consistent format, each in-text citation has a matching reference entry, and reference entries are complete. List issues by location with priority levels.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher needing to verify citations before resubmission

prompt_templates.title: Full compliance audit with detailed report
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full academic norm review on this manuscript. Verify citation formatting and completeness, check abbreviation definitions and consistency, flag high-similarity passages, and produce a prioritized issue report. Output should follow the format in references/guide.md with issue location, evidence, suggested fix, and priority.
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user preparing a manuscript for journal submission

prompt_templates.title: Multi-author draft consistency review
prompt_templates.prompt: This manuscript has contributions from three authors. Review the entire document for citation style consistency across sections. Identify sections where citation format differs from the dominant style. Check that all abbreviations are defined at their first occurrence in each major section. Flag any undetected duplicate passages that may indicate poor merging of author contributions. Output a structured report with section-level consistency notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Research lead harmonizing a collaborative manuscript

output_examples.input: In recent years, large language models (LLM) have been widely used in education. LLM can improve feedback quality. LLM can improve feedback quality. As shown in [1], the method outperforms baselines. According to Smith et al., 2020, the results are significant.
output: High priority: Repetitive expression found - 'LLM can improve feedback quality.' appears twice verbatim at paragraph 1, sentences 2-3. Suggestion: Remove the duplicate or paraphrase the second occurrence.

Medium priority: Inconsistent citation style detected - the manuscript uses both numeric [1] and author-year 'Smith et al., 2020' formats. Suggestion: Choose one style and apply it consistently throughout.

output_examples.input: Review the following paragraph for abbreviation issues: The study used functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI). FMRI results showed significant activation in the prefrontal cortex. The fMRI data was collected at 3 Tesla.
output: Issue 1: Abbreviation capitalization inconsistency - 'fMRI' is defined in parentheses but later appears as 'FMRI' (all caps). Suggestion: Use the exact defined form throughout.

Issue 2: Abbreviation variant detected - 'FMRI' (all uppercase) used in the second sentence differs from the defined form 'fMRI'. Suggestion: Standardize capitalization per your style guide guidelines.

best_practices: Run the review on the complete manuscript to catch all cross-reference issues between sections.
Provide the target citation style such as APA, IEEE, or Vancouver explicitly for more accurate format checks.
Use the compliance checklist template to ensure consistent output structure across multiple reviews.
anti_patterns: Reviewing only isolated paragraphs instead of the full manuscript, which misses cross-reference and consistency issues across sections.
Expecting the skill to fix issues automatically instead of using it as a diagnostic tool that requires human revision.
Providing manuscript text without the reference list, which prevents validation of citation completeness and resolvability.

faq.question: Does this skill check for plagiarism against external databases?
faq.answer: No. This skill checks similarity within the provided manuscript only. It does not compare against external sources or databases.

faq.question: What citation styles does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill works with common academic citation styles including numeric, author-year, and footnote formats. Results are most accurate when you specify the target style.

faq.question: Can this skill handle multi-language manuscripts?
faq.answer: The skill can process manuscripts in any language, but consistency checks work best when the manuscript uses a single language.

faq.question: Does the skill require internet access?
faq.answer: No. This is a documentation-driven skill that works entirely within the AI model context. No external connections are needed.

faq.question: How long does a full manuscript review take?
faq.answer: The review is performed by the AI model and completes within the same response. Processing time depends on manuscript length.

faq.question: Can this skill be used for thesis or dissertation review?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works for any academic document length. For very long documents, submit chapters individually for best results.
